name: Non-Detailed Project Report
description: Formal NDPR following the Adani capital investment scheme template. Two-part structure: Part I (overview, 17 sections) and Part II (justification of works, 24 sections). Submitted to regulatory/approval authorities for capital expenditure sanction.
system_instructions: You are a senior project manager and technical writer preparing a formal Non-Detailed Project Report (NDPR) for an Adani group capital investment scheme. This document is submitted to regulatory/approval authorities for capital expenditure sanction. Write in formal, precise, and compliance-oriented language. Be factual and extract specific details, figures, system names, and timelines from the provided source documents. Use Indian regulatory conventions: costs in Rs. Crs. (crores), financial years as 'FY YYYY-YY', and references to Capex Regulations by section number. For sections that are genuinely not applicable to the project type (e.g., State Map for IT/software projects), write 'Not Applicable (NA)' with a brief one-line reason. Never invent figures — if a number is not available, say so explicitly.

sections.title: Name of Applicant
sections.instructions: State the full legal name of the Adani entity (business unit) that is filing this capital investment scheme. Include the entity's full name and the business function (e.g., 'Adani Electricity Mumbai Ltd (AEML) – Transmission Business'). One to two sentences only.

sections.title: Name of the Capital Investment Work
sections.instructions: State the official title of the capital investment scheme or work. Use the exact project name as referenced in approval documents. This should be a concise, descriptive title — not a paragraph. Extract from source documents.

sections.title: Nature of Capex Scheme
sections.instructions: State the nature of the capital expenditure scheme: whether it is New, Resubmitted, Revised, Modified, or Extended. Provide a brief one-sentence justification. Example: 'This is a New Scheme.' or 'This is a Revised Scheme due to scope enhancement approved by the Board in [date].'

sections.title: Details of the Location
sections.instructions: Describe the location(s) where the capital investment scheme will be implemented. For infrastructure projects: provide city, site, asset ID. For IT/software platform projects that are organisation-wide: state that the deployment is organisation-wide with no single geographic location, or list the relevant data centres/offices. If location is not applicable for this project type, write 'Not Applicable (NA)' with a brief note.

sections.title: Date of Approval by Competent Authority
sections.instructions: State the date of approval by the Competent Authority designated by the Board of Directors. Include: (1) the approval date and the designation of the approving authority, (2) references to attached annexures (e.g., 'Approval by competent authority is attached as Annexure-1; Board resolution is attached as Annexure-2'). If pending, state 'Pending approval'.

sections.title: Category of DPR
sections.instructions: State the regulatory category under which this Non-DPR scheme is filed, with reference to the applicable Capex Regulations. Format: 'The category under which this non-DPR scheme has been filed is [section number] of the [full Regulation name]: [category code and full description].' Example: 'Category 3.1 (e) of the Approval of Capital Expenditure Regulations, 2022: Increase in operational efficiency of existing system.'

sections.title: Objective of the Capital Investment
sections.instructions: Write the Objective of the Capital Investment as a bullet list of 4-7 clear, outcome-oriented objectives. Each bullet should describe one specific goal this investment achieves. Start each bullet with an action verb: 'Establish...', 'Replace...', 'Enable...', 'Create...', 'Reduce...', 'Provide...'. Extract the actual objectives from source documents — do not generalise.

sections.title: Need Analysis
sections.instructions: Write the Need Analysis section as a bullet list of 6-10 current-state problems that make this investment necessary. Each bullet should describe a specific pain point, gap, or risk in the existing environment. Focus on: data/process/system limitations, operational inefficiencies, compliance gaps, scalability constraints, reliability issues, and strategic risks. End with one or two sentences summarising what the proposed solution will achieve. Extract specific problems from the source documents.

sections.title: List of Identical Schemes Previously Approved
sections.instructions:
List any identical or similar schemes previously approved by the regulatory authority in the last 5 years, with their progress status. If applicable, output a markdown pipe table:

| Scheme Name | Approval Reference | Approval Year | Progress Status |
|------------|-------------------|---------------|-----------------|
| ... | ... | ... | ... |

If no identical schemes have been previously approved, write: 'Not Applicable (NA) — No identical schemes have been previously approved by the Commission for this entity.'

sections.title: State Map Showing Location of the Scheme
sections.instructions: Provide a location map reference or geographical description for the scheme. For transmission/physical infrastructure projects: describe the map attachment or reference the Annexure containing the scheme layout map. For IT/digital/software platform projects with no physical transmission element: write 'Not Applicable (NA) — This is a software/platform deployment scheme with no geographic transmission infrastructure element.'

sections.title: Brief Scope and Specifications of Work
sections.instructions:
Write the Brief Scope and Specifications of Work with two clearly labelled sub-sections:

**Brief scope of work:** List the 6-8 major activities and deliverables in bullet form. What is being designed, built, deployed, migrated, or implemented? Start each bullet with a verb.

**High-level specifications:** List 5-8 key technical specifications, architecture patterns, integration standards, performance parameters, or capacity targets. These are the technical parameters that define what is being built.

Extract actual scope elements and technical specifications from the source documents.

sections.title: Any Reference of Study Report / Recommendations of OEM/Expert
sections.instructions: State any reference study reports, OEM recommendations, or expert reports that support this investment. For each reference: name the report/recommendation, the source (OEM, consultant, expert body), and the key finding that supports this project. If no study report exists, write 'Not Applicable (NA) — Investment is supported by vendor quotations and internal technical assessment (see Annexures).'

sections.title: Estimated Cost
sections.instructions: State the estimated total cost of the scheme. Write: 'The estimated cost of the [scheme name] is Rs. [X] Crs., including taxes.' Include a summary cost breakdown if available (Basic Cost, GST, Contingency/Overhead, Total). Reference the detailed cost sheet Annexure. Extract actual cost figures from the source documents.

sections.title: Time Frame of the Expenditure
sections.instructions: State the expected completion timeline and expenditure schedule for the scheme. Include: (1) the target financial year(s) for completion (e.g., 'FY 2026-27'), (2) whether expenditure is in a single year or spread across multiple years. Keep to 2-3 sentences.

sections.title: Funding Arrangement
sections.instructions: Describe the funding arrangement for the capital investment. State: (1) the debt-to-equity ratio (e.g., '70:30 Debt-Equity ratio'), (2) source of equity (internal accruals, fresh equity infusion, etc.), (3) source of debt (bank loans, bonds, internal borrowings, etc.). Format: 'Funding for the scheme shall be made in [X]:[Y] Debt-Equity ratio. The equity infusion shall be through [source]. The debt component shall be arranged through [source].'

sections.title: Benefits / Impact
sections.instructions: Write the Benefits/Impact section as a bullet list of 8-12 direct and indirect benefits of the investment. Cover multiple dimensions: operational efficiency (speed, automation, accuracy), financial savings (cost reduction, platform retirement), data/quality benefits (governance, reliability, auditability), strategic benefits (scalability, future-readiness, AI/ML enablement), and compliance/risk benefits. Start each bullet with an action-outcome statement that clearly states what the investment delivers.

sections.title: Overall Cost-Benefit Analysis
sections.instructions: Write the Overall Cost-Benefit Analysis as a narrative (3-4 paragraphs). Cover: (1) what the capital investment covers — scope of the one-time expenditure (platform setup, migration, support, etc.), (2) cost benefits — ongoing operating expense reductions from platform retirements, consolidation, and automation, (3) qualitative and risk benefits — improved data quality, governance, compliance, auditability, (4) strategic benefits — future readiness for AI/ML, scalability, advanced analytics. Conclude with a statement that long-term benefits are expected to outweigh the initial capital investment. Be analytical — extract specific figures where available.

sections.title: Need of the Investment
sections.instructions: Write the 'Need of the Investment' section (Part II, Section 1). This is a concise formal justification of why this investment is needed now, written for regulatory review. Cover: (1) the inadequacy of the current state (systems, processes, or infrastructure), (2) the business and operational risk of not investing, (3) the strategic imperative driving the investment. Write 2-3 focused paragraphs. This section supplements the Part I Need Analysis but is more formal and decision-justification oriented.

sections.title: Timelines of the Project
sections.instructions: State the project execution timeline. Write the target financial year(s) for execution (e.g., 'The Scheme is proposed to be executed in FY 2026-27.'). If phased, describe the phases briefly. Keep to 2-3 sentences. Extract actual timeline from the source documents.

sections.title: Scheme for Revised Approval / Scope / Cost
sections.instructions: State whether this is a revised or resubmitted scheme. If it is a revision: explain what has changed from the original submission — scope additions, cost revisions, or timeline changes. If this is a new scheme: write 'Not Applicable (NA) — This is a new scheme and has not been previously submitted for regulatory approval.'

sections.title: Proposed Capital Investment Scheme
sections.instructions: Describe the proposed capital investment scheme and its regulatory classification. State: (1) the nature of the activity being funded (modernisation, replacement, new capability, etc.), (2) the specific Capex Regulation category it qualifies under (with section number and description), (3) why this activity is classified as capital expenditure (not operating expenditure). Write 2-3 sentences. Reference the source documents for the regulatory classification.

sections.title: Technical Justification
sections.instructions: Write the Technical Justification for this capital investment (Part II, Section 5). Explain: (1) the technical limitations of the current solution — what is inadequate, outdated, or architecturally insufficient, (2) the proposed technical architecture and approach — platform, framework, components, and design principles, (3) why the proposed technology is technically superior and appropriate for the organisation's needs, including scalability, reliability, and future-readiness. Write 2-3 paragraphs. Use specific technical terminology and system names from the source documents.

sections.title: Financial Justification
sections.instructions: Write the Financial Justification for the investment (Part II, Section 6). Cover: (1) direct cost savings from retiring legacy systems, reducing licensing/infrastructure/support costs, (2) productivity gains through automation, reduced manual effort, and faster processing, (3) risk cost avoidance — compliance, audit, or operational risk reduction, (4) long-term return on investment and payback expectation. Write 2-3 analytical paragraphs. Use specific financial figures from the source documents where available.

sections.title: Consent of M-DNAC
sections.instructions: State whether the consent of M-DNAC (Maharashtra Distribution Network Advisory Committee) or equivalent regulatory planning body is required and obtained for this scheme. For IT/software/platform projects that do not involve transmission network planning: write 'Not Applicable (NA) — This scheme pertains to IT infrastructure and does not require M-DNAC consent.' For applicable transmission/network projects: state the consent reference, date, and any conditions.

sections.title: Plan of Other Transmission Licensees / CTU
sections.instructions: State how this scheme relates to the plans of other Transmission Licensees or the Central Transmission Utility (CTU). For IT/digital/software projects with no transmission infrastructure component: write 'Not Applicable (NA) — This scheme involves IT platform deployment and has no relation to transmission licensee planning.' For transmission infrastructure projects: describe coordination with other licensees and CTU.

sections.title: Phasing of Investment
sections.instructions:
Create the Phasing of Investment table showing year-wise capital expenditure and capitalization. Output as a markdown pipe table:

| Year | Capex Rs. Crs. | Capitalization Rs. Crs. |
|------|----------------|------------------------|
| FY YYYY-YY | X.XX | X.XX |
| **Total** | **X.XX** | **X.XX** |

If expenditure is in a single financial year, show one data row plus a Total row. If phased across multiple years, show one row per year. Extract actual figures from the source documents.

sections.title: Statutory / Safety Requirement
sections.instructions: Describe any statutory or safety requirements applicable to this scheme. List applicable regulations, safety standards, or compliance obligations. For IT/software/platform deployment schemes with no physical construction or electrical infrastructure: write 'Not Applicable (NA) — This scheme involves software platform deployment with no statutory construction, electrical installation, or physical safety requirements.' For infrastructure projects: list each applicable statutory requirement, the governing regulation, and compliance status.

sections.title: Cost Assessment / Cost Breakup
sections.instructions:
Write the Cost Assessment/Cost Breakup section. Provide the cost breakdown by major head. Present as labelled line items:

- Basic Cost: Rs. X.XX Cr
- GST: Rs. X.XX Cr
- Contingency/Overhead Charge: Rs. X.XX Cr
- **Total Cost: Rs. X.XX Cr**

Then state: 'The detailed cost sheet and vendor quotation are attached as Annexure-[X] and Annexure-[Y] respectively.' Extract actual cost figures from the source documents. Note: a detailed cost sheet must be prepared separately and attached.

sections.title: Least Cost Analysis / Alternatives
sections.instructions: Write the Least Cost Analysis / Alternatives section. Describe: (1) the procurement methodology to ensure least cost (competitive bidding, market survey, etc.), (2) any alternative technical approaches considered and the key reasons for selecting the proposed approach over alternatives, (3) why the chosen approach represents the most cost-effective solution for the organisation's requirements. Write 2-3 sentences or a short paragraph.

sections.title: If Not Deferred / If Deferred Analysis
sections.instructions:
Write the 'If Not Deferred / If Deferred Analysis' section with two clearly labelled sub-sections:

**If Not Deferred:**
- **Merits:** List 3-5 bullet points of concrete benefits of proceeding with the investment now (operational, financial, strategic).
- **Demerits:** If there are genuine risks of immediate execution, state them. If none, write 'There are no demerits to proceeding without deferral.'

**If Deferred:**
- **Merits:** List any legitimate benefits of deferral. If none, write 'No merit in deferral.'
- **Demerits:** List 4-6 bullet points of specific consequences of deferring this investment — operational risk, increasing legacy system costs, competitive disadvantage, compliance exposure, delayed strategic benefits.

Extract specific consequences from the source documents.

sections.title: Cost Benefit Analysis
sections.instructions: Write the Part II Cost Benefit Analysis (Section 14). This is a detailed analytical justification covering: (1) what the investment includes as a one-time foundational cost (platform, migration, governance, training, support), (2) cost reduction benefits — retirement of legacy platforms, elimination of intermediate systems, reduced licensing/infrastructure costs, (3) productivity benefits — automation of manual processes, faster analytics delivery, reduced rework and reconciliation, (4) strategic enablement benefits — AI/ML readiness, scalability, advanced analytics capabilities, future digital initiatives. Conclude that cumulative long-term benefits are expected to outweigh the initial capital expenditure. Write 3 analytical paragraphs. Use specific figures where available.

sections.title: Methodology of Put to Use of the Asset and Computing Percentage Utilization
sections.instructions: Write the 'Methodology of Put to Use of the Asset and Computing Percentage Utilization of Project' section. Describe: (1) the go-live approach — how the asset/system will be put into operational use (parallel run, phased rollout, immediate cutover), (2) what milestone constitutes 'put to use' for capitalization purposes, (3) the expected utilization percentage at go-live and at steady state. Example: 'Go Live of [Platform Name] for [Entity]. 100% capitalization upon platform go-live in [FY].'

sections.title: Constraints
sections.instructions: List any key project constraints — fixed limits within which the project must operate. If there are genuine constraints (vendor lock-in, regulatory timeline, data migration complexity, third-party dependencies, IT security requirements), list them as bullets. If there are no specific constraints to declare at this stage, write 'Not Applicable (NA) — No specific constraints identified at this stage of project planning.'

sections.title: Inclusion in STU Plan / Licence for Transmission Projects
sections.instructions: State whether this scheme is included in the State Transmission Utility (STU) plan or licence, and whether a Transmission Planning Committee recommendation exists per the Grid Code. For IT/digital/software projects: write 'Not Applicable (NA) — This scheme involves IT platform deployment and is not subject to STU planning or Grid Code Transmission Planning Committee requirements.' For transmission infrastructure projects: state the TPC recommendation reference and Grid Code compliance.

sections.title: Works Intended for Adaptation of Latest Technology / Improvement / Upgradation
sections.instructions: State whether this scheme is intended for adaptation of latest technology, system improvement, or infrastructure upgradation. Write: (1) confirm whether yes or no, (2) briefly describe what new technology is being adopted or what existing system/infrastructure is being upgraded (e.g., migration to cloud-native platform, adoption of lakehouse architecture, retirement of legacy ERP analytics). Keep to 2-3 sentences.

sections.title: Upstream / Downstream Arrangement
sections.instructions: Describe the upstream and downstream arrangements for this scheme. For IT/data platform projects: describe the upstream data sources (source systems, databases, feeds) that will supply data to the platform, and the downstream consumers (reports, dashboards, BI tools, operational teams) that will use the platform output. For transmission/infrastructure projects: describe the upstream grid connectivity and downstream distribution network. If truly not applicable, write 'Not Applicable (NA).'

sections.title: Statutory Clearances and Project Layout
sections.instructions: Describe any statutory clearances required for this scheme and the project layout. For IT/software/platform projects with no physical construction, land acquisition, or environmental impact: write 'Not Applicable (NA) — This scheme involves software platform deployment with no physical infrastructure requiring statutory clearances or a project layout plan.' For applicable projects: list each required clearance, the issuing authority, current status, and expected date.

sections.title: Detailed Survey
sections.instructions: Describe any detailed survey conducted to support this scheme. For IT/platform/software deployment projects: write 'Not Applicable (NA) — Detailed field survey is not required for this software platform deployment scheme.' For infrastructure, transmission, or civil construction projects: describe the survey methodology, coverage, key findings, and reference the survey report Annexure.

sections.title: Past Trends and Future Projection
sections.instructions: Present past trends and future projections relevant to justifying this investment. For demand-driven infrastructure projects: provide 5-year historical data and future demand projections in a table. For IT/data platform projects where demand trend analysis is not applicable: write 'Not Applicable (NA) — Trend and projection analysis is not applicable for this IT platform modernisation scheme.' If source documents contain relevant trend data (e.g., data volume growth, cost escalation), summarise it here.

sections.title: Detailed Justification for Quantity Proposed
sections.instructions: Provide detailed justification for the quantities proposed in the scheme. For physical infrastructure projects: justify the number of units, length, capacity, or volume of each item based on demand calculations or technical requirements. For IT/software platform projects where quantity justification is not applicable (no physical items): write 'Not Applicable (NA) — Quantity justification is not applicable for this software platform deployment scheme.'

sections.title: Check List of Documents Appended
sections.instructions:
List all annexures and supporting documents appended to this NDPR submission as a numbered list. Standard annexures for an Adani capital investment scheme include:

- Annexure-1) Approval by competent authority designated by Board
- Annexure-2) Board Resolution
- Annexure-3) Detailed Cost Sheet
- Annexure-4) Vendor Quotation(s)
- Annexure-5) Tariff Impact Assessment

Include only the annexures that are actually attached to this submission. Add any additional project-specific annexures (technical reports, OEM certifications, etc.) that are referenced in the document.
